refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall provide an integrated capability to derive performance requirements from worksite mission context (site conditions, task objectives, safety constraints), record derived requirements with traceability to source inputs, and maintain a managed baseline history including calibration updates and performance target revisions.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct functional concerns: derivation (analysis/synthesis), recording (data capture), and management (lifecycle control). Refinement separates these into analyzable sub-functions while maintaining traceability to worksite inputs and baseline management as acceptance criteria. High safety criticality demands explicit linkage to safety constraints in derivation logic.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall demonstrate: (1) a documented derivation method that maps worksite inputs (site conditions, task type, safety plan) to performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability); (2) a recorded baseline with full traceability; (3) a change history showing at least two calibration/update cycles with audit trail; (4) confirmation that safety-critical performance targets are flagged and reviewed by Safety Manager.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance requirements from worksite mission context inputs (site conditions, task objectives, safety constraints).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance requirements with full traceability to source worksite inputs and derivation method.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed baseline history of performance targets, including calibration updates and revision audit trail.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Ensure safety-critical performance requirements are identified, documented, and reviewed by Safety Manager.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement a centralized performance requirements database with a derivation engine that accepts worksite context inputs (site conditions, task type, safety plan) and outputs performance targets; maintain versioned baselines with change tracking and audit logs.

DP-B: Distribute performance derivation logic across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, with a shared data store for baseline management; each module derives targets relevant to its domain and synchronizes via a common interface.

DP-C: Implement a lightweight configuration-driven approach where performance targets are pre-defined templates matched to worksite context via a lookup service; baselines stored in a simple versioned file system with manual audit trail.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Implement a centralized performance requirements database with a derivation engine that accepts worksite context inputs (site conditions, task type, safety plan) and outputs performance targets; maintain versioned baselines with change tracking and audit logs.

potential_sources: Coupling to worksite mission context inputs (site conditions, task objectives, safety plan) is necessary and intentional; this is the problem domain.
Coupling to Safety Services for safety constraint validation is necessary and appropriate for high safety criticality.
Coupling to Telemetry interface for recording actual performance against derived targets is necessary for baseline management.
No unnecessary coupling to Vehicle or Teleoperation control logic; derivation is decoupled from execution.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits necessary coupling to inputs and safety validation, but maintains logical separation between requirement derivation (analysis) and requirement execution (control). This is a clean separation of concerns.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context inputs (site conditions, task type, safety plan) are available and well-defined at the time of performance requirement derivation.
ASSUMPTION: Safety Manager is available to review and approve safety-critical performance targets before deployment.

RISK: If worksite context is incomplete or ambiguous, derivation engine may produce invalid or unsafe performance targets. Mitigation: Implement validation rules and require explicit approval for safety-critical targets.
RISK: Centralized database becomes a single point of failure for performance management. Mitigation: Implement redundancy and backup mechanisms.
RISK: Derivation engine logic may be complex and difficult to validate. Mitigation: Use model-based requirements engineering and formal verification for safety-critical derivation rules.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-A is sound. Recommend adding explicit specification of: (1) the derivation algorithm/rules (e.g., lookup tables, regression models, expert system); (2) the validation and approval workflow for safety-critical targets; (3) the baseline versioning and change control process.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module owns the centralized performance requirements database, derivation engine, baseline management system, and audit trail. This module integrates worksite mission context inputs, interfaces with Safety Services for constraint validation, and exposes performance targets via Tasking interface to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution outputs derived performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) and baseline metadata to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules via Tasking interface.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety constraints and worksite safety plan from Safety Services; sends safety-critical performance targets for review and approval; receives validation feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives actual performance telemetry (cycle time, energy consumption, availability metrics) from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to compare against derived targets and update baselines.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link to communicate with Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality stakeholders for baseline review, calibration updates, and historical data access.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural owner of system-level performance requirement derivation, baseline management, and audit trail. This is a core system-level capability that should not be distributed. High containment score reflects that all derivation logic, database, and versioning should reside here. Low diffusion score indicates minimal leakage to other modules.

Vehicle module is responsible for executing performance targets, not deriving them. Placing derivation logic in Vehicle would couple requirement analysis to vehicle-specific implementation details and prevent reuse across different vehicle types. High diffusion score reflects that this capability does not belong in Vehicle.

Teleoperation module is responsible for remote operation control, not system-level performance requirement derivation. Placing derivation logic in Teleoperation would create unnecessary coupling and prevent reuse. High diffusion score reflects that this capability does not belong in Teleoperation.

Safety Services is responsible for safety constraint validation and safety-critical review, not performance requirement derivation. While Safety Services should validate and approve safety-critical targets, the derivation engine itself should remain in NGHE Solution. Moderate containment score reflects that Safety Services provides input validation, not ownership of derivation logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate owner of performance requirement derivation, recording, and baseline management because: (1) this is a system-level capability that must be independent of specific vehicle or teleoperation implementations; (2) it requires integration of worksite mission context inputs from multiple sources; (3) it must maintain a single source of truth for performance baselines and audit trails; (4) it interfaces with Safety Services for safety-critical validation, not as a subordinate function but as a peer validation partner.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution becomes too large or monolithic, it may be difficult to maintain and evolve. Mitigation: Implement clear internal module boundaries within NGHE Solution (e.g., derivation engine, database, audit trail) with well-defined interfaces.

RISK: If worksite mission context inputs are not reliably available to NGHE Solution, derivation may fail or produce invalid targets. Mitigation: Implement input validation and fallback mechanisms; require explicit confirmation of worksite context before derivation.

RISK: If Safety Services validation is slow or unavailable, performance target deployment may be delayed. Mitigation: Implement asynchronous validation workflow; allow provisional targets pending safety review.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: IM assignment is sound. Recommend: (1) define internal module structure within NGHE Solution (derivation engine, database, audit trail as sub-components); (2) specify the interface contract for worksite mission context inputs; (3) define the safety validation workflow and approval process; (4) specify the baseline versioning and change control process.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (centralized performance requirements database with derivation engine and baseline management) directly and sufficiently addresses all aspects of the refined FR: (1) derivation from worksite mission context, (2) recording with traceability, (3) managed baseline history with calibration updates, (4) safety-critical identification and review. The DP is logically sound, decouples requirement analysis from execution, and maintains necessary coupling to inputs and safety validation. No coupling issues identified.

diffusion_risks: LOW RISK: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner; no diffusion to Vehicle or Teleoperation. Derivation logic remains centralized and reusable.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution achieves 95% containment score for this capability. The module boundary is clear: NGHE Solution owns derivation, recording, and baseline management; Vehicle and Teleoperation own execution and telemetry reporting. Interfaces are well-defined (Tasking for output, SafetyAlert for validation, Telemetry for feedback, Network Link for stakeholder access). No significant containment risks.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: HIGH: The refined FR is verifiable through: (1) demonstration of a documented derivation method with worksite input mapping; (2) inspection of recorded baselines with traceability; (3) review of change history and audit trail; (4) confirmation of safety-critical target identification and Safety Manager review. All verification activities are feasible within NGHE Solution module scope.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: HIGH: The allocation of CR-NGHE-005 to NGHE Solution is clear and unambiguous. Sub-FRs (derivation, recording, baseline management, safety review) are all allocated to NGHE Solution with clear interfaces to Safety Services and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules. Ownership and responsibility are explicit.

recommended_revisions: REVISION 1: Specify the derivation algorithm/rules in detail (e.g., lookup tables, regression models, expert system rules) to enable validation and verification.
REVISION 2: Define the safety validation workflow explicitly, including approval authority, review criteria, and escalation procedures.
REVISION 3: Specify the baseline versioning and change control process, including who can initiate changes, approval requirements, and audit trail format.
REVISION 4: Define the interface contract for worksite mission context inputs, including required data elements, format, and validation rules.

decision.justification: The refined FR is sound, the selected DP is appropriate and well-justified, and the IM assignment to NGHE Solution is correct with high containment and low diffusion. All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass. The requirement is ready for decomposition to the next level. The four sub-FRs identified in the refined FR (derivation, recording, baseline management, safety review) should be decomposed further to define the detailed design of the derivation engine, database schema, audit trail mechanism, and safety validation workflow.
decision.justification_detail: This is a complex system-level requirement that benefits from further decomposition. The sub-FRs are not yet atomic and require detailed design specifications. Proceeding to the next level will enable: (1) detailed specification of the derivation algorithm and rules; (2) definition of the database schema and baseline versioning mechanism; (3) specification of the safety validation workflow and approval process; (4) detailed interface specifications for worksite context inputs and performance target outputs.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance requirements from worksite mission context inputs (site conditions, task objectives, safety constraints).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance requirements with full traceability to source worksite inputs and derivation method.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed baseline history of performance targets, including calibration updates and revision audit trail.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Ensure safety-critical performance requirements are identified, documented, and reviewed by Safety Manager.

assumptions: Worksite mission context inputs (site conditions, task type, safety plan) are available and well-defined at the time of performance requirement derivation.
Safety Manager is available to review and approve safety-critical performance targets before deployment.
Vehicle and Teleoperation modules will report actual performance telemetry to NGHE Solution for baseline comparison and calibration.
Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality stakeholders will access baseline history and calibration updates via Network Link interface.
The derivation algorithm/rules can be formalized and validated (e.g., lookup tables, regression models, expert system rules).
open_risks: RISK: If worksite context is incomplete or ambiguous, derivation engine may produce invalid or unsafe performance targets. Mitigation: Implement validation rules and require explicit approval for safety-critical targets.
RISK: Centralized database becomes a single point of failure for performance management. Mitigation: Implement redundancy and backup mechanisms.
RISK: Derivation engine logic may be complex and difficult to validate. Mitigation: Use model-based requirements engineering and formal verification for safety-critical derivation rules.

RISK: If NGHE Solution becomes too large or monolithic, it may be difficult to maintain and evolve. Mitigation: Implement clear internal module boundaries within NGHE Solution with well-defined interfaces.

RISK: If Safety Services validation is slow or unavailable, performance target deployment may be delayed. Mitigation: Implement asynchronous validation workflow; allow provisional targets pending safety review.
evidence_needed: Documented derivation method/algorithm with worksite input mapping and performance target output specification.
Database schema design for performance requirements baseline and version history.
Safety validation workflow specification, including approval authority, review criteria, and escalation procedures.
Interface specifications for worksite mission context inputs (format, required data elements, validation rules).
Interface specifications for performance target outputs via Tasking interface.
Audit trail format and change control process specification.
Verification plan for derivation algorithm validation and safety-critical target review.
Traceability matrix linking CR-NGHE-005 sub-FRs to detailed design specifications.
